steps.text: Distribute the subtraction sign across the second parenthesis: $(9c + 8) - (4c^3 - 6) = 9c + 8 - 4c^3 + 6$.
Combine the constant terms: $8 + 6 = 14$. The linear term $9c$ and cubic term $-4c^3$ have no like terms to combine.
Write the result in descending degree order: $-4c^3 + 9c + 14$.

Evaluate all choices:
  • A: $4c^3 + 9c + 2$ — the cubic term has a positive sign (should be negative) and constant is wrong. Eliminated.
  • B: $4c^3 + 9c + 14$ — the cubic term has a positive sign (should be negative). Eliminated.
  • C: $-4c^3 + 9c + 2$ — cubic term and linear term are correct but the constant should be 14, not 2. Eliminated.
  • D: $-4c^3 + 9c + 14$ — all three terms match exactly. That matches choice D — D is correct.
